title: pellaea nivea f. tenera
intro: Pellaea nivea f. tenera is a rare and endemic species of fern, specifically a subspecies of the Pellaea nivea plant, commonly found in the high-altitude regions of New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pellaea nivea f. tenera include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote tissue regeneration, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
